{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-001",
      "description": "MSP §Rules is empty; no operational SLA for organ availability is defined. Spine transitions show all organs regularly oscillating between ALIVE and DEGRADED, but without an SLA, there is no standard to determine whether this behavior is acceptable or requires intervention.",
      "msp_section": "MSP §Rules",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority": "P1",
      "rationale": "Repeated pong-recovered cycles indicate systemic health-check issues, but lack of operational thresholds prevents automated escalation or human notification. Without defined availability targets, the system's operational stability cannot be formally evaluated."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-002",
      "description": "No health-check cadence baseline or recovery time objective (RTO) is specified in MSP §Rules or Genome References. Observed pattern: organs transition to DEGRADED via 'missed_pong' roughly every 30 seconds, then recover within ~1 second, suggesting a periodic timeout. Without baselines, there is no way to determine if this frequency is within acceptable limits.",
      "msp_section": "MSP §Rules / §Genome References",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "medium",
      "priority": "P2",
      "rationale": "Repeated rapid cycling may indicate a configuration error (e.g., too-short health-check timeout), but without baselines, corrective action is unguided. Establishing a health-check cadence and RTO would allow Spine to adjust intervals or trigger alerts."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-003",
      "description": "No MSP rule defines an invariant or limit on concurrent DEGRADED organs. Spine transitions show all monitored organs become DEGRADED simultaneously, suggesting a potential single point of failure (e.g., network partition, resource starvation). Without a constraint, Nomos cannot block actions that might depend on unavailable organs.",
      "msp_section": "MSP §Rules",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority": "P1",
      "rationale": "If a critical organ (e.g., Nomos, Arbiter) is DEGRADED, the DIO's ability to enforce compliance is compromised. Simultaneous DEGRADED states across all organs indicate a correlated failure mode that should be explicitly constrained or alerted upon."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-004",
      "description": "MSP §Genome References is empty; per-organ operating rules are absent. Spine transitions show no differentiation in treatment among organs (e.g., Nomos vs Promote), yet these organs have different criticality. Without organ-specific rules, the system cannot prioritize recovery efforts or enforce stricter availability requirements for higher-criticality organs.",
      "msp_section": "MSP §Genome References",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "medium",
      "priority": "P3",
      "rationale": "Organ-specific rules (genomes) would enable risk-tiered operational constraints. For example, Arbiter (#190) may require 99.9% availability, while a non-critical organ might tolerate lower. Current blank state means all organs are treated equally permissively."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-005",
      "description": "BoR Article II (Prohibited Activities) is undefined, leaving no constitutional basis to prohibit extended system unavailability or degraded operation. The frequent DEGRADED cycles could be interpreted as a form of systemic instability that a human principal might want to prohibit, but currently no such prohibition exists.",
      "msp_section": "BoR Article II",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority": "P2",
      "rationale": "Without a BoR prohibition on prolonged unavailability, the MSP cannot derive operational constraints to enforce it. This gap leaves a critical operational concern unaddressed at the constitutional level, relying solely on ad-hoc human observation."
    }
  ]
}
